project_context.name: LLM Visibility Checker

stack.frontend: Next.js 14+ (App Router, Tailwind CSS, Shadcn UI)

project_context.goal: Create a tool similar to Google PageSpeed Insights that checks if a URL is known, cited, and correctly classified by major LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Perplexity).

instructions.step_1_architecture: Plan the database schema in Laravel to store crawl history, scores for different models, and recommendation strings.
instructions.step_2_api_logic: Design a service class in Laravel that sends prompts to LLMs (e.g., 'What do you know about [URL]?', 'Is [URL] a reliable source for [Topic]?') and parses the responses.
instructions.step_3_frontend_ui: Draft a Next.js dashboard using Shadcn UI components. Use a 'Gauge Chart' for the overall visibility score and 'Progress Bars' for individual model presence.
instructions.step_4_recommendations: Implement a logic that compares LLM responses with on-page SEO data to generate actionable 'Improvement Areas' (e.g., lack of structured data, inconsistent brand mentions).

include: API Controller logic for LLM querying
Mockup of the scoring algorithm (LLM Visibility Index)
